originalTitle: The bottleneck might be the air in the room
content:
You gather your most expensive people into a room to make your most important decisions. Then, somewhere in the second hour, the room quietly gets worse at making them. Not the people. The room.

I now travel with a portable CO 2 monitor. Outdoors it reads around 400 parts per million. In a closed meeting room with a handful of people in it, I have watched it climb past 2,000. The photo here is a real reading: 2,143.

That number matters more than it looks. Researchers at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory put people in a chamber and varied only the CO 2 . At 1,000 ppm, performance dropped significantly on six of nine decision-making measures compared with a clean-air baseline of 600. At 2,500 ppm, seven of the nine fell substantially, some into a range they called dysfunctional. A separate study out of Harvard found cognitive scores declining as CO 2 rose, with the steepest losses in exactly the domains you called the meeting for: strategy, planning, and using information under pressure.

Here is the uncomfortable part. 1,000 ppm is not an extreme number. A closed room with a few people breathing in it reaches that inside the first hour. Your all-day planning session, your architecture review, your quarterly strategy offsite in the windowless boardroom: those are precisely the conditions that push CO 2 into the range where decision quality measurably falls. You are running your highest-stakes thinking in the environment least suited to it.

And it is invisible from inside. Nobody in the room feels impaired. They feel a little tired, a little foggy, a little checked out, and they put it down to the length of the meeting, a bad night’s sleep, or the person who won’t stop talking. The one variable almost nobody checks is the air.

This is not only a boardroom problem. With so much work now remote, your people spend their days in small home offices with the door shut. Same physics, same climb, same afternoon fog. The dip your team hits mid-afternoon may owe less to motivation than to a room that hasn’t exchanged its air since morning.

A few years ago, one client tried to use this as an argument for bringing everyone back to the office. They touted how much better the building’s air was than anything people had at home. So I brought the monitor and it was eye-opening. Some parts of the building were genuinely as good as outdoor air; plenty were not. The meeting rooms were still a problem, and the more people in an area, the worse it got.

I’ve spent decades understanding why capable teams underperform, and I have learned to be suspicious of any explanation that starts by blaming the people. Before you conclude that the team is disengaged, that they can’t think strategically, or that the meeting culture is broken, it is worth ruling out the cheapest variable in the building. A CO 2 monitor costs less than an hour of your time. Opening a window or a door costs nothing.

You already instrument your build pipeline, your cycle time, your defect rates. You measure the systems your people work inside because you know the environment shapes the output. The air in the room is part of that environment, and right now it is the one input you are not measuring.

I learned this the memorable way once, by sealing my own team into a room full of CO 2 as a Halloween stunt . The everyday version is far less dramatic and far more common.

Open a window. Then watch what happens to the second half of the meeting.
chineseTitle: 瓶颈可能是房间里的空气
chineseContent:
你把最昂贵的人聚集到一个房间里，做出最重要的决定。然后，在第二个小时的某个时候，房间的制作速度悄然变得更糟。不是人民。房间。

我现在旅行时会携带便携式 CO 2 监测仪。户外读数约为百万分之 400。在一个只有少数人的封闭会议室里，我亲眼目睹人数突破了 2000 人。这里的照片是真实读数：2,143。

这个数字比看起来更重要。劳伦斯伯克利国家实验室的研究人员将人们放入一个房间中，仅改变 CO 2 。与清洁空气基线 600 相比，在 1,000 ppm 时，九项决策指标中的六项性能显着下降。在 2,500 ppm 时，九项决策指标中的 7 项大幅下降，其中一些指标进入了他们称之为功能失调的范围。哈佛大学的另一项研究发现，认知得分随着 CO 2 的上升而下降，其中损失最严重的正是您召开会议的领域：战略、规划和在压力下使用信息。

这是不舒服的部分。 1,000 ppm 并不是一个极端的数字。一个只有几个人呼吸的封闭房间在第一个小时内就达到了这一点。您在没有窗户的董事会会议室中进行的全天规划会议、架构审查、季度战略：这些正是将 CO 2 推入决策质量明显下降范围的条件。你正在最不适合的环境中进行最高风险的思考。

而且从内部看它是看不见的。房间里没有人感到受到损害。他们感觉有点累，有点迷茫，有点失落，他们把原因归结为会议时间太长、晚上睡不好，或者是那个不停说话的人。几乎没有人检查的一个变量是空气。

这不仅仅是一个董事会问题。由于现在有如此多的工作处于远程状态，您的员工每天都关着门呆在小型家庭办公室里。相同的物理原理，相同的攀登，相同的午后雾。你的团队在下午三点左右的表现下降可能不是因为动力不足，而是因为房间从早上起就没有换过空气。

几年前，一位客户试图以此作为让所有人回到办公室的理由。他们吹嘘大楼的空气比人们家里的空气好得多。所以我带了显示器，它让我大开眼界。建筑物的某些部分确实与室外空气一样好；很多都没有。会议室仍然是一个问题，而且一个区域的人越多，情况就越糟糕。

我花了几十年的时间来理解为什么有能力的团队表现不佳，并且我学会了对任何从指责员工开始的解释持怀疑态度。在你得出团队不敬业、无法进行战略思考或会议文化被破坏的结论之前，有必要排除大楼中最便宜的变量。一台 CO 2 监测仪花费的时间不到一个小时。打开窗户或门不需要任何费用。

您已经检测了您的构建管道、周期时间和缺陷率。您可以测量员工在其中工作的系统，因为您知道环境会影响输出。房间里的空气是该环境的一部分，现在它是您没有测量的输入。

我曾经以令人难忘的方式学到了这一点，将我自己的团队密封在一个充满 CO 2 的房间里作为万圣节特技。日常版本的戏剧性要小得多，而且更为常见。

打开一个窗口。然后观察会议后半段会发生什么。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
